rejected_candidates.title: Hardcoded external addresses are used without chain or code validation
rejected_candidates.reason: This only becomes an issue if the contract is deployed on the wrong chain or an unusual fork; on the intended mainnet deployment it is not a permissionless exploit, so it is better treated as deployment misconfiguration than a reportable vulnerability.

rejected_candidates.title: Counter state is fully mutable by any caller
rejected_candidates.reason: `Counter` is an isolated toy contract with no assets, privileged flows, or in-scope integrations. Public writes are its entire visible behavior here, so this is not a meaningful security finding in this codebase.
